translated: ProSeka's "Unsteady"... I totally get it.
Kon-needo! I'm Yuki Nakashima, the voice of Shiho Hinomori,
and I'll be your host for this ProSeka AfterTalk.
We're going to look back at the event...
or at least, that's what the script says!
The title "Unsteady, Still Step" is such a tongue twister, I can't say it well at all.
Did I get it? It's a real mouthful!
Anyway, thank you for joining me to look back at the story today.
So, about "Unsteady, Still Step"... as you all saw, Honami was...
It was a story about being lost and struggling to make a big decision.
Reading through it, I really felt like, "Yeah, things like this definitely happen."
Did you like the story?
Ah, thank you so much.
I'm glad.
Starting from April, AfterTalk has had a mini-renewal, and we've been collecting messages
via the official ProSeka Discord and email in advance.
Thank you all for your messages!
I'll be introducing some of the messages we received during the show,
so look forward to that.
Also, please post your thoughts on the event or this broadcast using the hashtag #ProSekaAfterTalk.
I'm also watching the YouTube chat, so I'll be picking up comments as we go.
Additionally, with the renewal, the segment where we watch the AfterLive together has ended,
so please keep that in mind.
Right, there was a choice that had to be made precisely because they're professionals.
Yeah.
Shindo-san was such a reliable person.
That's true.
In that scene where they're on the phone, how should I put it?
In the direction where Honami and Shindo-san are on screen, you could really see Shindo-san's great posture,
and it made me realize all over again that he's a mature adult man.
The AfterLive was great too.
Mhm.
Thank you very much.
All right, let's get right into the story of "Unsteady, Still Step."
There really are a lot of "steps" in this title.
I might end up just talking to myself the whole time!
I'd love to discuss it with you all.
Let's start by introducing some messages.
First, we have a message from King-san.
Thank you.
Nakashima-san, kon-needo!
Kon-needo!
In this story, I could see Leoni growing more and more as professionals,
which made me really happy.
Both the solo live and the fan festa are chances for Leoni to take a big step forward,
so it's hard to decide whether to write a new song when there's no right answer.
I'm not good at making decisions and often get stuck overthinking,
so I really empathized with Honami's feelings.
By the way, Nakashima-san, how do you usually reach a conclusion when making a major decision?
Thanks for writing in.
Thank you!
Hmm, let's see.
I'm usually the type who has a clear answer decided, but in a case like this story,
if I want to do both, I'll just try to do both.
But if the schedule has jobs on the exact same day,
then I obviously have to pick one. However, if it's like this time,
where the deadlines just overlap and I'm wondering what to do, I'd probably just take it on.
So it really depends on what the decision is,
but I'm generally the type who decides things pretty decisively.
And after that, I just let my emotions take over!
So I'm probably not much help as a reference, sorry!
What about all of you?
On the flip side.
Yeah, if I can do both, I want to do both.
I know that's being selfish, though.
Yeah.
That's how it is.
Letting emotions decide.
Mhm.
The only way is to draw lots.
True.
Well, if you'll have no regrets regardless of which one you pick,
then leaving it to luck might actually be a good idea when you can't decide.
I feel like doing a "which one should I pick" game is fine too.
Hmm, but if I really could only do one...
I'd go with my gut feeling.
Whichever way I'm leaning more.
Yeah, even if I'm leaning just a few percent toward one side, I'd choose that one.
Right.
But as for the content of the story this time, well...
